questions.question: Explain the bootstrap process in operating systems. What are the key steps involved?
keyPoints: Power on and execution of POST (Power-On Self-Test)
Bootstrap program loaded from ROM/EEPROM
Initialization of hardware components
Loading of operating system kernel into memory
Starting of system services and creation of user environment
Display of login prompt or desktop environment

questions.question: What is dual-mode operation in operating systems? Why is it important?
keyPoints: Definition of kernel mode and user mode
Kernel mode has full hardware access privileges
User mode has restricted access to hardware
Importance for system protection and security
Prevents user programs from directly accessing hardware
Enables OS to protect critical system resources

questions.question: Describe the different types of interrupts in operating systems.
keyPoints: Hardware interrupts from external devices
Software interrupts or traps caused by errors
System calls as software interrupts for service requests
Interrupt handling mechanism and priority
Role of interrupt controller in managing multiple interrupts
Difference between synchronous and asynchronous interrupts

questions.question: What are system calls? List and explain the main categories of system calls.
keyPoints: System calls provide interface between user programs and OS kernel
Process control system calls (fork, exec, wait, exit)
File management system calls (open, read, write, close)
Device management system calls (read, write, seek)
Information maintenance system calls (getpid, getuid)
Communication system calls (pipe, socket)
Protection system calls (chmod, setuid)

questions.question: Explain the role of device drivers in operating system operations.
keyPoints: Device drivers act as intermediaries between OS and hardware
They translate generic OS commands to device-specific instructions
Driver runs in kernel mode with full privileges
Types of drivers: character, block, network devices
Hot-swappable driver loading and unloading
Driver abstraction hides hardware complexity from applications

questions.question: What is the Process Control Block (PCB)? What information does it store?
keyPoints: PCB is a data structure maintaining process information
Process state (new, ready, running, waiting, terminated)
Program counter indicating next instruction
CPU registers and general purpose registers
Memory management information (page tables, limits)
I/O status information and open files
Accounting information (CPU time used)

questions.question: Explain the relationship between operating system and application programs.
keyPoints: OS provides platform for application execution
Manages hardware resources on behalf of applications
Provides system calls and API for program development
Applications request services through system calls
OS ensures fair resource allocation among applications
Isolation between applications for security and stability

questions.question: Explain the concept of interrupt handling in operating systems. Discuss the interrupt handling mechanism and the steps involved when an interrupt occurs.
keyPoints: Definition and purpose of interrupts
Types of interrupts (hardware, software, traps)
Role of interrupt controller (PIC/APIC)
Steps: interrupt detection, saving CPU state
Identification of interrupt source and priority
Execution of appropriate interrupt handler
Returning from interrupt and restoring state
Interrupt-driven I/O vs programmed I/O
Interrupt latency and handling efficiency

questions.question: Discuss the structure of an operating system. Explain the various approaches to organizing operating system components.
keyPoints: Simple monolithic structure (all in one layer)
Layered approach with defined interfaces between layers
Microkernel architecture with minimal core functions
Hybrid approach combining different architectures
Client-server model for distributed systems
Virtual machine concept and implementation
Comparison of approaches with advantages and disadvantages

questions.question: What are the main functions of an operating system? Explain each function in detail.
keyPoints: Process Management: creating, scheduling, terminating processes
Memory Management: allocating and deallocating memory space
File Management: creating, deleting, organizing files
Device Management: handling I/O devices through drivers
Security and Protection: controlling access to resources
Networking: managing network connections and protocols
User Interface: command-line or graphical interface
Error Detection and Recovery: handling system failures

questions.question: Explain what happens when a user executes a program. Discuss how the operating system loads and manages program execution.
keyPoints: User requests program execution through interface
OS locates the executable file on storage
Creating new process and allocating PCB
Loading program into memory (memory management)
Setting up address space and initializing stack
Preparing program counter and initial registers
Transition from kernel to user mode
CPU scheduling the process for execution
Handling system calls during execution
Process termination and resource cleanup
